meaning: adj.锡耶纳的 锡耶纳人的
examples: Raphael's final and most prominent patron, the Sienese banker Agostino Chigi, had just been married to one Francesca Ardeasca.
Ucello painted the battle between Florentine and Sienese forces in 1432 - which took place within his lifetime - three times, each time employing spectacular color and - for the time - great realism.
Indeed, the Sienese place incredible demands on the national TV network, RAI, for rights to televise their festival.
Il Palio is one of very few surviving medieval spectacles of its type in Italy, enduring thanks to the sheer tenacity of Sienese traditionalism.
